text: These YC companies are on fire

quoted_tweet.text:
Three Warp customers just announced massive rounds.

Reducto - $108M raised from a16z and Benchmark. Teaching AI to read documents. Processing 1B pages monthly.

Greptile - $25M Series A led by Benchmark. Reviews 500M+ lines of code monthly. Prevents bugs before production for Brex, PostHog, Substack.

Datacurve - $15M Series A. Building bounty hunter systems for the AI training data nobody else can get.

Congrats to all three teams. Let's keep building.

thread: These YC companies are on fire
reply: it’s always a little wild to me how 99% of the world will never even hear about companies like this but they just quietly grease the wheels of progress behind the scenes

text:
You come across a tweet that tells you that everything is going to work out. How does it make you feel? If it's able to generate even the slightest bit of positive emotion in you, then it's literally true

Because if it wasn't, you wouldn't have reacted that way. You would've felt nothing or flat out rejected it. Proves that something in you still believes it's not the end of your story, that you still embody enough faith for your body to have not rejected the white pill. You have nothing to worry about
thread:

Proves that something in you still believes it's not the end of your story, that you still embody enough faith for your body to have not rejected the white pill. You have nothing to worry about
reply:
optimism as a physiological reflex test LOL love it
but also: you can make yourself feel hopeful over time by flooding ur brain w feel good propaganda. it actually does sink in over time. You have to simultaneously admit to yourself u don't fully buy it yet but really really want to

text:
It's really hard to lose money if you enjoy your life. Making it was never the issue. Be a driven person and invest part of your income every month. You really don't have much to worry about. You'll see anywhere between a lot and a ton of it, just stay alive

Problem was always feeling like you needed to escape your mind. Contribute something purposeful to society, share meaningful experiences with people you love, your headspace suddenly turns into a safe place to call home

Now you're thinking "well I'm happy, life is good, so do I really need to fomo into this thing? Is this really my one chance to win? Am I really missing out on anything?" Answer is no, because you feel like you've already reached the final destination in the present

This sense of internal security allows you to capitalize on opportunities from a place of abundance, rather than scarcity. Assessing risk is now backed by curiosity, rather than anxiety. You begin to see yourself as someone who already won, where the passing of physical time will just make you more and more aware of that

Why feel the need to retire when you're actually living? Why do you care so much about hitting this particular number now when it's essentially a given later in the decade? Why sabotage it all for a chance to chase something that isn't nearly as fulfilling as what you currently have?

Embody the right energy, trust that you'll always be fine, and showcase gratitude when luck surprises you with realities that allow you to relish in being more than fine. Not needing to be anywhere other than "here" is what gives the world permission to bring you "there"
thread:

Now you're thinking "well I'm happy, life is good, so do I really need to fomo into this thing? Is this really my one chance to win? Am I really missing out on anything?" Answer is no, because you feel like you've already reached the final destination in the present

This sense of internal security allows you to capitalize on opportunities from a place of abundance, rather than scarcity. Assessing risk is now backed by curiosity, rather than anxiety. You begin to see yourself as someone who already won, where the passing of physical time will just make you more and more aware of that

Why feel the need to retire when you're actually living? Why do you care so much about hitting this particular number now when it's essentially a given later in the decade? Why sabotage it all for a chance to chase something that isn't nearly as fulfilling as what you currently have?

Embody the right energy, trust that you'll always be fine, and showcase gratitude when luck surprises you with realities that allow you to relish in being more than fine. Not needing to be anywhere other than "here" is what gives the world permission to bring you "there"
reply: this is beautifully said tbh. the best ROI in life is rearranging your own headspace until being you feels like a good place to be. it's wild how abundance starts to show up everywhere once you're not urgently searching for it

text:
how tf do i get my package with UPS in the US?  

they refuse to give it to me except if i'm home, and they randomly tell me in the middle of the day "hey you need to be home between now and 5 hours forward". wtf?? and hold at ups location is "ineligible"??

text:
I thought that sentence was pretty self explanatory but what I mean is before i got the corp job i thought it would be gay & i’d hate it but it was actually fun af, got paid 6-figs for a job i did on literal autopilot & got off at 2:30pm, had all the homies there, and was “the guy”

Experiencing that made me realize exactly why people never leave. It hits every paychological human need at the exact same time lmao. U have to be lowkey sick in the head & actually about ur growth to bail on that. If i didn’t have the desire to turn my yearly income into my monthly income i would have never bailed. But soul overtook comfort & i needed aventure+risk+fun. I knew my potential was way higher than the world’s smoothest/safest 6 figures

A lot of life actually works this way. You think you’ll hate something (and will probably tell people how gay it is) BEFORE you even actually experience it yourself. then when you ACTUALLY experience it, You’re like “oh I get it”

Once i went through this experience I stopped making judgements on things I had never directly experienced because I was very aware how much of an asshat that made me

“Corp jobs are gay bro I could never get a corp job!” - guy that’s never had a corp job

quoted_tweet.text: @retirementkeys “Before i got that job i thought it would be gay then when i left i 100% understood why most never do” what do you mean by this?

thread:
I thought that sentence was pretty self explanatory but what I mean is before i got the corp job i thought it would be gay & i’d hate it but it was actually fun af, got paid 6-figs for a job i did on literal autopilot & got off at 2:30pm, had all the homies there, and was “the guy”

Experiencing that made me realize exactly why people never leave. It hits every paychological human need at the exact same time lmao. U have to be lowkey sick in the head & actually about ur growth to bail on that. If i didn’t have the desire to turn my yearly income into my monthly income i would have never bailed. But soul overtook comfort & i needed aventure+risk+fun. I knew my potential was way higher than the world’s smoothest/safest 6 figures

A lot of life actually works this way. You think you’ll hate something (and will probably tell people how gay it is) BEFORE you even actually experience it yourself. then when you ACTUALLY experience it, You’re like “oh I get it”

Once i went through this experience I stopped making judgements on things I had never directly experienced because I was very aware how much of an asshat that made me

“Corp jobs are gay bro I could never get a corp job!” - guy that’s never had a corp job

I will also say there was monstrous identity pain for me being associated with literal incapable emotionally-stunted normietards

I didn’t give a fuck about the fact iw as getting paid more / was “better”, me doing the SAME THING as some retarded bitch doing drugs in the bathroom while casting spells on me in her free time made a part of me hate myself. It’s like “what the fuck is you doing buddy” type shit lmfao. If u settle for bottomfeeder-accessible shit you’ll have always a little bottomfeeder alive inside of you

Some dude will shart himself while drinking a big gulp and you’ll be like “the fact we’re both in the same place is fucking absolutely astronomical”. And you’re actually right lol. U playing way 2 small but avoiding the work required to shoot upwards.

Get on that magic carpet kang. Spread dem wings @ fly 🆙🆙🆙

You also get psychefucked by the hiveprogramming. Unless ur psyche is strong enough to snap spells you’ll probably get fucked mentally

I had failed a company & jumped in with ambitious & take-over-world energy. I literally asked sales manager in my interview “if one car is going 120mpg while all others are going 35pmh will the 120mph car get paid more”. I knew i was the best & it would just be bloodshet on scoreboard. I just needed cash for my next diceroll

But when u actually enter into it it’s infinite social proof/assumption that you’re literally retarded. You are IN the cage of the babies, and it is endless subconscious programming that tells you “hey you’re retarded!”. But it’s not for YOU specifically, it’s because you’re with people that LITERALLY need it.

The guy sharting himself from his 9th big gulp & the single mom with 3 baby daddys & a drug problem actually do need it at a 1st grade level. That is why i got offended at first. And then when i realized the truth i was like “woah”

You really do have to hate yourself to put up with that in a way - with rare exceptions
reply:
honestly, a lot of this just reads as “I’m too special for my environment” cope. totally get the shock of realizing you actually fit in, or even thrive, in a setting you once scorned, corporate can be scarily cozy. but if being “the guy” makes you spiral about being surrounded by normies, that’s just a new flavor of ego trap. 

the humility pill is realizing how easy it is for *anyone* (no matter how smart or “different”) to become seduced by comfort. stuff like “bottomfeeder alive inside you” is like, bro… it’s all of us. the people you’re sneering at are just earlier in the loop.

text:
If you post anything genuinely truthful/helpful you’ll quickly realize how monstrous the % of people are that are ABSOLUTELY fucking cooked IS, based on their own voluntary willingness to stay cooked. Totally self-prescribed, entirely willingly

“Nobody understands me or believes in me!”. No shit. They are literally all nondiscerning retards afraid of literally everything unimportant (including you rising). Just tune it out.

Basically nobody “gets it”. If someone does get it - that is SO fucking rare it’s genuinely astronomical when you find someone that does. Ur on some mad max type shit. “Oh, you have a soul? Hi”

Don’t hinge your psychological or spiritual health on “does the group accept me”. If THAT is your aim, you are DOOMED to be a perpetually caged, uninteresting loser who just copes & replyguys through life as he pokes people from the anon shadows

Instead hinge ur psyche on “did i courageously fade the bullshit around me to pursue what I was pulled to pursue on my life & generate my actions based on the map God placed into MY soul. Did i give MYSELF permission to go MY WAY..”

Hinge it on if your ACTIONS are aligned with your own SELF. If the ACTIONS are in agreement with your soul

If THAT answer is yes, you WILL be okay. 

You’ll probably have a lot of mouthbreathers mad/coping at you & firing off little soyremarks 24/7 as they attack ur sweg, but who gives a fuck

“Is the path i’m walking aligning with my soul or not”.

And it takes $0 to start there (shifting the foundational alignment/path your life is going). And even if you “don’t know what to do”, you KNOW where to look & where to start. You KNOW what to “pivot” your life towarda to “make it feel more nourishing/fun/enjoyable”. What sounds fucking fun to you? Go do that. 

Out of MIND, into HEART

reply:
peak "if you know you know" energy.  
people need to learn to filter for resonance over volume, if 99% of ppl “don’t get it” it just means you’re finding your tribe, not that you’re crazy.  
the real test is if you can keep going before anyone claps for it

text: how to solve hard problems easily, explained by one of the world's best competitive programmers: https://t.co/XEoicnfVIB
thread: how to solve hard problems easily, explained by one of the world's best competitive programmers: https://t.co/XEoicnfVIB
reply:
love this breakdown. basically: intuition is pattern matching + motivated repetition. 
the “always verify your intuition with logic” part is slept on, so many smart people get wrecked bc their intuition is good but not calibrated. 
growth mindset in a nutshell: train the right patterns, not just grind for grind’s sake.

text:
this is crazy. an upcoming 1,000-page O'Reilly book is already No. 1 🥇 in "computer hardware & architecture" on Amazon... the longest book in O'Reilly's history!

AI Systems Performance Engineering covers how the hardware and software in real-world AI systems go hand in hand.

1. Introduction and AI System Overview
2. AI System Hardware Overview
3. OS, Docker, and Kubernetes Tuning
4. Tuning Distributed Networking Communication
5. GPU-based Storage I/O Optimizations
6. GPU Architecture, CUDA Programming, and Maximizing Occupancy
7. Profiling and Tuning GPU Memory Access Patterns
8. Occupancy Tuning, Warp Efficiency, and Instruction-Level Parallelism
9. Increasing CUDA Kernel Efficiency and Arithmetic Intensity
10. Intra-Kernel Pipelining and Cooperative Thread Block Clusters
11. Inter-Kernel Pipelining and CUDA Streams
12. Dynamic and Device-Side Kernel Orchestration
13. Profiling, Tuning, and Scaling PyTorch
14. PyTorch Compiler, XLA, and OpenAI Triton Backends
15. Multi-Node Inference Parallelism and Routing
16. Profiling, Debugging, and Tuning Inference at Scale
17. Scaling Disaggregated Prefill and Decode
18. Advanced Prefill-Decode and KV Cache Tuning
19. Dynamic and Adaptive Inference Engine Optimizations
20. AI-Assisted Performance Optimizations

@cfregly was previously an engineer leader at AWS and Databricks. he definitly knows what he's talking about. I've also seen so many positive reviews, such as @rasbt's. 

I haven't been this excited about any other book for a while, because this one points fingers at a pain-point that is rarely ever discussed, and is super comprehensive about it too! 

you can already pre-order the book, before it is released next month: https://t.co/y21jkSxrDk

this post is not sponsored. I'm genuinely excited about this, and you should probably also be.

1,000 pages is insane. imagine how many lifetimes you’d need to deeply grok all of it lol. 
we’re living through “the manual gets thicker every year” in real time, feels like. but also kinda cool, proof there’s still real technical depth to be had if you want it.

text: 7 years ago, OpenAI was a cool company making AI that plays Dota, works on robotics, and RL. fast forward to 2025, and we're talking about a new browser or selling ads. my apologies if I'm not impressed anymore. https://t.co/JgR42zapcR
thread: 7 years ago, OpenAI was a cool company making AI that plays Dota, works on robotics, and RL. fast forward to 2025, and we're talking about a new browser or selling ads. my apologies if I'm not impressed anymore. https://t.co/JgR42zapcR
reply:
honestly all tech companies start off “playing” and end up “optimizing for revenue” it’s the nature of the beast  
the golden age is always like, the first 2-3 years where it’s weird and experimental and no one’s thinking abt ads yet

quoted_tweet.text:
An exciting milestone for AI in science: Our C2S-Scale 27B foundation model, built with @Yale and based on Gemma, generated a novel hypothesis about cancer cellular behavior, which scientists experimentally validated in living cells. 

With more preclinical and clinical tests, this discovery may reveal a promising new pathway for developing therapies to fight cancer.

reply: growing up i read sci fi novels about artificial intelligence making actual scientific discoveries.  now it's real! awe-inspiring and also little unnerving

thread:
Prompting and evals will be everything

Prompting is agency

reply: yep, love this framing. prompting = your ability to get leverage out of the system, evals = whether you know what’s actually worth optimizing for. most ppl have neither.

text:
Real market alpha isn’t loud confidence; it’s the capacity to stay in contact with fear long enough to read the signal instead of flinching from it. 

The market punishes comfort. The founders and investors who separate themselves are the ones who can distinguish “this feels dangerous because it’s stupid” from “this feels dangerous because it will rewire my map.”
thread:
Real market alpha isn’t loud confidence; it’s the capacity to stay in contact with fear long enough to read the signal instead of flinching from it. 

The market punishes comfort. The founders and investors who separate themselves are the ones who can distinguish “this feels dangerous because it’s stupid” from “this feels dangerous because it will rewire my map.”
reply: yesss, every actual edge is found in the uncomfy spots where your brain is screaming “abort mission!!” but it’s actually just unfamiliar territory. wish we spent as much time learning to metabolize fear as we do optimizing for certainty
